results.title: ADP GenAI Engineer Interview Questions & Guide 2026
highlights:
At **ADP**, a **GenAI Engineer** sits at the intersection of cutting-edge artificial intelligence and massive-scale human capital management. **ADP** processes payroll and benefits for over 40 million workers globally, meaning any AI solution deployed here has an immediate, tangible impact on the lives of millions. As a **GenAI Engineer**, you will design, build, and scale the next generation of AI-powered assistants, co-pilots, and natural language processing (NLP) systems that simplify complex HR and payroll tasks for clients and employees alike.
...
This role is highly collaborative and strategically vital. You will partner closely with product management, UX designers, and platform teams to transform raw technology into robust, enterprise-grade products. Whether you are optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines, integrating conversational AI with contact center platforms like **Salesforce** or **Genesys**, or building secure middleware to process sensitive data, your work will directly shape the future of work.
...
### Generative AI & NLP Concepts

Preparing for an interview at **ADP** requires a balanced strategy. You must demonstrate both your foundational software engineering skills and your specialized expertise in Generative AI. The evaluation process is designed to see if you can write clean code, explain your past work clearly, and design scalable AI architectures.
...
**Role-Related Knowledge** – You must demonstrate a deep understanding of modern NLP and GenAI technologies. This includes practical experience with LLMs, vector search, prompt engineering, and evaluation frameworks. Interviewers want to see that you understand the underlying mechanics, not just how to call APIs.
...

As a **GenAI Engineer** at **ADP**, your primary responsibility will be building and maintaining the AI infrastructure that powers **ADP**'s client-facing and internal tools. You will not work in isolation; your day-to-day tasks will involve heavy collaboration across multiple departments to deliver robust, secure, and highly scalable AI solutions.
...

...
* **Integrate Enterprise Systems**: Connect GenAI applications with third-party contact center platforms like **Genesys** and **Salesforce**, ensuring low-latency communication and smooth data transfers.
...

results.title: Melissa N.
highlights:
Lead Data Scientist at ADP |LLMs, Retrieval Systems, Multilingual NLP, GenAI | I Empower Teams with Human-Centric |I Build AI Products that Reduce Effort & Elevate Accuracy
...
I lead enterprise AI development that transforms how teams access, process, and act on knowledge. My focus is on building scalable, multilingual NLP and retrieval systems that improve accuracy, reduce manual effort, and deliver measurable business value. From custom lexicons and domain-specific LLM tools to forecasting and classification apps, I specialize in end-to-end AI solutions that scale across organizations. At ADP, I manage a cross-functional team that creates automation pipelines powering call summaries, chatbot intelligence, and internal knowledge graphs. These tools are used daily by thousands of associates and have significantly increased retrieval precision and workflow efficiency. I bring a results-driven mindset to education and civic analytics, developing future talent and designing data-driven products that support social impact. What motivates me most is the intersection of AI and people: building systems that not only solve complex problems but also empower others to do more.
...
### Lead Data Scientist at [ADP](
...
://www.
...
.com/company/adp) (Current)
...
I direct a team of data scientists, ontologists, and engineers that builds enterprise-grade NLP and ML tools that drive smarter support, faster automation, and more intuitive enterprise search. My work blends deep tech, such as LLMs, retrieval systems, and lexicons, with practical results that impact customers, agents, and internal teams. NLP & SPEECH SYSTEMS ➤ Improved transcription accuracy from 30% to 90% for 80+ ADP domain terms by developing a tailored lexicon and judge model using LLMs. ➤ Eliminated manual summary writing for calls and chats across four languages (EN/ES/FR/NL) by deploying GPT pipelines for multilingual automation. KNOWLEDGE, RAG & ONTOLOGIES ➤ Raised article retrieval relevance to 94% and retrieval accuracy by 20% for Service Assist by integrating knowledge graphs and ontologies into the AI pipeline. ➤ Deployed document retrieval solutions for Time Labor and Management (TLM), enhancing support experience and reducing lookup times. AGENT ASSIST &CHATBOTS ➤ Increased chatbot accuracy and reduced agent handoffs by launching an utterance classifier for Payroll chatbot workflows. ➤ Reduced workflow errors by auto-identifying issue types in transcripts and recommending matching knowledge base articles. results.title: Abhijit Choudhary
highlights:
Seasoned Data Scientist | Gen AI | NLP · NLU · LLM | IEEE Keynote Speaker | IIT & Georgia Tech Alum | Heroic Award @ ADP | Language Tech: N-grams to Agents | Global Experience: USA · Singapore · India
...
REAL WORLD ENTERPRISE LEVEL MODERN MACHINE LEARNING AT SCALE: As part of ADP's next-gen payroll platform initiative, I play a key role in building scalable AI/ML pipelines to automate the "vedor agnostic" digitization of payroll documents—turning unstructured PDF data into accurate, structured formats used across enterprise systems. I am proud to spearhead the below capabilities: ■ Development of modular Gen-AI/NLP pipelines to extract and validate data from legacy payroll PDFs—automating a previously manual onboarding workflow leading to significant reduction in turnaround time. ■ Google Doc AI integration, including fine-tuning extractor guidelines, optimizing training for out-of-box accuracy, and layering custom validation models to mitigate hallucinations. ■ Researched and applied the optimal stack of language technologies—evaluating GenAI models (Sonnet 3.5, GPTs, LLaMa, Mistral), fine-tuning transformers (e.g., BERT), and developing custom embeddings. ■ Built a synthetic document generator to simulate high-volume, diverse payroll documents—accelerating model training while eliminating exposure to PII. ■ Designed scalable text classifiers and section/page labeling algorithms to categorize diverse PDF formats with high precision. ■ Text Properties-Based Extraction: Effectively segregates semantic information from any PDF document, enabling a variety of downstream ML algorithms. ■ Developed custom NER models tailored to noisy, form-based document structures—capturing fields like SSN, Address, and Generational Suffix with consistency. ■ Created a robust validation framework to identify extraction issues at the token level and trigger early alerts for business users.. ■ Partnered cross-functionally with product, engineering, and cloud infrastructure teams to deploy ML solutions seamlessly within enterprise workflows. Show less
...

...
CONSULTANT DRIVING TRANSFORMATIVE NLP TECH INNOVATIONS FOR FORTUNE 500 CLIENTS: _______________________________________________________________________________________________________ ■ Clients Served: Worked on several greenfield innovation projects. Successfully served several market leaders like ADP, Wells Fargo, JPMC, FINRA in their AI journey (NLP Focus). ■ Data Scientist building state of the art machine learning capabilities for leading Fortune 500 clients with focus on Natural Language Processing (NLP/NLU) to resolve business problems in the area of: Contextual Disambiguation, Semantic Search, Information Extraction, Custom NER, and Novelty Detection (Extremely Rare Events). ■ Entrepreneurial Experience: Operated as an independent consultant during this period, allowing me to handpick challenging, innovative projects and hone my technical expertise while simultaneously developing entrepreneurial skill. This experience helped me blend a sharp technical focus with a deep understanding of business needs, adding immense value to clients' AI journeys. ■ Proficient in building machine learning pipelins to prototype and identify champion model and then articulate the benfits to business. ■ Leveraged major frameworks and technologies including: Tensor flows, kerras, Scikit Learn, SBERT, HuggingFace, USENC, knowledge graph, nltk, Spacy, Glove, word2vec, regex, Numpy, Pandas, Matplotlib, boto3, impyla, pyspark, AWS, HDFS, Databricks. ■ Enjoy to serve, delight and build relations with clients -working on cutting edge technologies in NLP/NLU space. ■ Served several market leaders across industry segments. results.title: Arka Chowdhury
highlights:
Lead Data Scientist at ADP
...
Data Scientist at [ADP
...
Lead Data Scientist at ADP working on implementing GenAI, ML and Statistics to analyze HRM and Payroll Data with the ultimate goal to reduce client support calls. As a Senior MLOps Engineer specialized in automating ML workflows, scalable deployment, and infrastructure-as-code. Built GitHub Actions pipelines to register models from AWS SageMaker to Databricks Unity Catalog (80% faster onboarding), designed CI/CD pipelines for Docker-based model deployment in Artifactory (70% faster releases), and developed Terraform-based infrastructure for OpenAI-powered endpoints in Databricks (weeks to hours provisioning with 99.9% uptime) As a Senior Data Scientist at GEP worked on GenAI use cases in Procurement, Supplier and Risk Management mainly chatbot development. I have developed an internal AutoML Platform using ensembling for classification and regression of data uploaded by users. I have also developed a demand planning tool for inventory management and stocking policy recommendations. At RSG Media Systems I developed optimization and prediction models to make lives easier in the media industry. Scheduling advertisements and promos to target audience is my forte. I am a graduate student from University at Buffalo with masters in Industrial and Systems Engineering. I had significant inclination towards Operations Research during my course there. I did my summer internship in NIT Rourkela where I worked on heuristic approach for optimizing job scheduling. I got interested in this field of Operations Research after having an enriching experience there.

Engineered a robust Anonymization API that detects and masks personally identifiable information (PII) in contract documents before processing with LLMs, enhancing data security and compliance with data protection regulations. This system uses LLMs to summarize and rate suppliers based on responses in questionnaire forms, effectively aiding in risk assessment and quick decision-making for procurement teams. Created a specialized chatbot for auctions, analyzing supplier data to provide key decision-making insights, improving customer engagement by 20% and decision accuracy during auction events. Configured Docker and Kubernetes environments to optimize GPU usage and ensure smooth application deployment. Activity: Sanjay Varma Rudraraju
...
One of the fun thing about reading new ML/AI papers is implementing them but with the crazy amount of papers that are being published these days it's just too time consuming. Luckily I came across a cool project that solves exactly this. CatalyzeX is a free browser extension that finds code implementations for ML papers and if it can't then it'll give you an easy way to reach out to the author to request the same. Check it out at https://www.catalyzex.com/ #machinelearning

results.title: INTELLIGENT DATA INGESTION - Patent Application
highlights:
Intelligent data ingestion is provided. A determined column header name of a selected
...
The disclosure relates generally to human capital management data and more specifically to intelligently ingesting client data into a database corresponding to a human capital management service provider using a plurality of machine learning models.
...
imported data file.
...
to the human capital management service.
...
In response to a client uploading a data file, illustrative embodiments map the uploaded data to appropriate data columns in the database of the human capital management service using a plurality of machine learning models. Illustrative embodiments train one of the machine learning models using a dictionary of data column header names. Illustrative embodiments utilize this column header name-based machine learning model to predict the appropriate column header name in the database of the human capital management service that matches a given column header name in the client's data file. For example, illustrative embodiments may utilize the column header name-based machine learning model to predict a data column header name when a prediction may be difficult (e.g., when selecting between first name, last name, nickname, or the like).
...
machine learning model is the
...
Subsequently, the computer selects a column from the plurality of columns in the imported data file (step 306). The computer maps a determined column header name of the selected column in the imported data file to a predicted corresponding column header name of a particular column in a database corresponding to a human capital management application using a plurality of machine learning models (step 308). The plurality of machine learning models may include, for example, a column name-based machine learning model, a data type-based machine learning model, and optionally a column proximity-based machine learning model.
...
Afterward, the computer makes a determination as to whether the predicted corresponding column header name output by each respective machine learning model of the plurality of machine learning models matches (step 310). If the computer determines that the predicted corresponding column header name output by each respective machine learning model of the plurality of machine learning models does match, yes output of step 310, then the process proceeds to step 316. If the computer determines that the predicted corresponding column header name output by each respective machine learning model of the plurality of machine learning models does not match, no output of step 310, then the computer generates a decision tree to determine which particular machine learning model of the plurality of machine learning models is best for predicting a corresponding column header name in the database corresponding to the human capital management application for the determined column header name of the selected column in the imported data file (step 312). The computer may utilize, for example, a decision tree algorithm to generate the decision tree. The decision tree algorithm is a supervised machine learning algorithm that continuously divides data based on a predefined set of rules until a final outcome is generated.
...
The computer identifies the predicted corresponding column header name output by the particular machine learning model of the plurality of machine learning models that is best for predicting the corresponding column header name in the database for the determined column header name of the selected column in the imported data file using the decision tree (step 314). The computer utilizes the predicted corresponding column header name of the particular column in the database as a target column name for the determined column header name of the selected column in the imported data file (step 316). The computer also makes a determination as to whether another column exists in the plurality of columns in the imported data file (step 318).

results.title: Jeremy
highlights:
# Gen AI Engineer
...
Design and develop large language models, generative AI systems, and NLP pipelines for a Fortune 25 healthcare company, with responsibility for end-to-end ML model lifecycle from development through production deployment.
...
### Position Title
...
Gen AI Engineer
...
### Job Description
...
Gen AI Engineer
...
The Gen AI Engineer is responsible for analyzing and modeling organizational data for the Artificial Intelligence (AI) function to draw business insights, which can be used to make business decisions.
...
### How You Will Make an Impact
...
- Applies data extraction, transformation and loading techniques in order to connect large data sets from a variety of sources.
...
- LLM development and fine-tuning strategies, best practices, and standards to enhance AI ML model deployment and monitoring efficiency.
...
- Develop roadmap and strategy for NLP, LLM, Gen AI model development and lifecycle implementation.
...
- Responsible for the design and development of custom ML, Gen AI, NLP, LLM Models for batch and stream processing-based AI ML pipelines including data ingestion, preprocessing modules, search and retrieval, Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), NLP/LLM model development and ensure the end-to-end solution meets all technical and business requirements, and SLA specifications.
...
- Work closely with the MLOps team to create and maintain robust evaluation solutions and tools to evaluate model performance, accuracy, consistency, reliability, during development, and UAT.
...
- Identify and implement model optimizations to improve system efficiency.
...
- Collaborate closely with the MLOps, product teams, business stakeholders, machine learning engineers, and software engineers for the deployment of machine learning models into production environments, ensuring smooth integration, reliability and scalability.
...
- Ensure the use of standards, governance and best practices in ML model development, and adherence to model and data governance standards.

### Minimum Requirements
...
- Requires a Bachelor’s degree in a highly quantitative field (Computer Science, Machine Learning, Operational Research, Statistics, Mathematics, etc.) or equivalent degree and 4 or more years of experience; or any combination of education and experience in configuration management, which would provide an equivalent background.
...
### Preferred Skills, Capabilities, and Experiences
...
- Advanced Python proficiency.
- 4+ years of professional hands-on experience leveraging large sets of structured and unstructured data to develop data-driven tactical and strategic analytics and insights using ML, NLP, and computer vision solutions.
- Demonstrated 4+ years hands-on experience with Python, SQL, Hugging Face, TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, and Spark.
- Experience with GCP/AWS cloud platforms.
- Strong knowledge of and measurable hands-on experience with developing or tuning Large Language Models (LLM) and Generative AI (GAI)
- Experience with NLP, LLMs (extractive and generative), fine-tuning and LLM model development.
...
- Experience developing and optimizing high-quality prompts for NLP applications.
...
- LLM Infrastructure & Deployment: LLM serving platforms (vLLM, Text Generation Inference, FastAPI); Model quantization for LLMs (GPTQ, AWQ, bitsandbytes); GPU memory optimization techniques (tensor parallelism, pipeline parallelism); LLM caching strategies for inference optimization; RAG architecture design and implementation.
...
- Advanced cloud infrastructure (AWS EKS/ECS, GCP GKE, Azure AKS) knowledge.
...
- Containerization strategies for ML workloads; Canary deployments for ML models.
...

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
...
# Data Scientist
...
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
...
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
...
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
...
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
...
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics, machine learning, simulation, and mathematical modelling
...
- Designs and delivers predictive and prescriptive models for optimal data value extraction
...
- Utilizes tools such as Python, R, SQL, Hadoop, and analytics platforms for large-scale data processing
...
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
...
- Collaborate with data engineers to design data pipelines and vector stores that support RAG architecture, embeddings, and efficient retrieval from internal knowledge bases
...
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
...
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for
...
teams, and
...
driven decision making
...
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
...
### Required Qualifications:
...
- Bachelor’s Degree
...
preferably in information technology

engineering or other

- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects

- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
...
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
...
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
